text:
Once upon a time, in the heart of a lush, verdant forest, there was a small but mighty oak tree named Oakley. Oakley was not just any old oak; it was a tree with a special gift—the ability to communicate with all the creatures that lived around it. This gift made Oakley the guardian of the forest, a place where all kinds of animals and plants thrived.

One sunny morning, as the first rays of light filtered through the leaves, a young rabbit named Thumper hopped over to Oakley. Thumper had a problem; a large storm was coming, and her burrow wasn't ready for the rain and wind. Oakley listened carefully, sensing the urgency in Thumper's voice.

"Thumper, don't worry," Oakley said, using its deep, resonant voice. "I will help you find a safe place to stay until the storm passes."

Oakley extended its roots, feeling out the ground beneath the forest floor. Soon
